page: Vultr vcg-a16-3c-32g-8vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with an x86_64 Intel CPU featuring 3 shared physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB per core. For acceleration, it includes a fractional 0.5x NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 170 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between CPU and GPU-accelerated workloads. The shared Intel CPU delivers average single-core performance in stress-ng tests, but multi-core and Passmark CPU scores fall into the weak category, reflecting the shared-core architecture and 2.0 GHz clock speed. Memory read and write bandwidth for large blocks are average, while small-block cached read bandwidth is weak. However, the fractional NVIDIA A16 GPU delivers strong performance in LLM inference benchmarks. Prompt processing for a 7B model reaches top-tier speeds of 600.63 tokens per second, and text generation for the same model achieves a strong rate of 27.65 tokens per second.

This server offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by utilizing a fractional GPU and shared CPU cores, avoiding the higher costs of dedicated full-GPU instances. The primary tradeoff is the weak multi-core CPU performance and limited memory bandwidth, which makes it less suitable for heavy CPU-bound computation or high-throughput web serving. It is best suited for light LLM inference, small-scale machine learning model deployment, and GPU-accelerated development environments.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-3c-32g-8vram is a Cloud GPU server designed for entry-level accelerated workloads. It features 3 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 32.0 GB of system memory, and a 170 GB NVMe SSD. Graphics and compute acceleration are handled by a fractional 0.5x NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. While CPU-bound benchmarks show weak multi-core performance, the GPU excels in machine learning tasks, delivering top-tier prompt processing speeds of 600.63 tokens per second on 7B LLM models. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective solution for developers needing GPU acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated card. It is highly suited for small-scale LLM inference, model prototyping, and GPU-assisted development.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-3c-32g-8vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 3 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 170 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 8 GB VRAM for efficient LLM inference.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-3c-32g-8vram features 3 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA A16 GPU with 8 GB VRAM for budget-friendly AI inference.
tagline: A budget-friendly cloud server combining shared Intel processors with fractional NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for efficient small-scale AI inference.

3 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for 7B LLM inference
Best suited for small-scale AI inference and GPU prototyping
